analysis_method.priority_extraction_method: Main manifesto sections identified from the contents page and the headline plan pages.
analysis_method.weighting_method: Page-span weighting across the manifesto’s substantive sections using the printed contents-page structure.
analysis_method.ranking_method: Rank combines section prominence, page-span and headline emphasis from the plan pages and foreword.
analysis_method.position_handling: Directions are inferred from the manifesto’s section framing; no strong anti-directional constitutional section is foregrounded in this model.

headline_plan: Cut NHS wait times with funding following the patient
End the 8am rush for a GP appointment
Bring back the family doctor
New technology in our NHS with an NHS app and AI scanners
A mental health emergency service
Top up tax free childcare and two weeks of summer holiday clubs
Breakfast clubs in every school
Ban mobile phones in classrooms
2,000 Education Recovery teachers and 1,500 more classroom assistants
9,000 new apprenticeships to deliver an apprenticeship guarantee
Abolish the unfair business rates system
End the block on clean nuclear energy
Deliver the Glasgow airport rail link
Community police officers for every neighbourhood
A £350m potholes fund to fix the roads
Fair funding for local communities
125,000 new homes to fix the broken housing market
End government waste and cut a third of quangos
A right to recall MSPs
More support for people trying to buy their own home

sources.description: First half of the headline plan
Second half of the headline plan
Contents page with section structure and printed page numbers

priorities.position: An NHS there when Scots need it
priorities.summary: Largest core service section in the manifesto, also strongly reflected in the headline plan.
A top-tier manifesto priority focused on affordability and everyday costs.

priorities.position: Homes for all; 125,000 new homes to fix the broken housing market
priorities.summary: A substantial dedicated section and one of the clearest headline commitments.

priorities.position: Setting our young people up to succeed
priorities.summary: Major manifesto section with strong headline commitments on schools and classrooms.
A substantial section tied to economic renewal, business rates reform, and growth.

priorities.position: Developing skills and creating good jobs
priorities.summary: Dedicated section on apprenticeships, skills and good jobs.

priorities.position: Stronger communities; fair funding for local communities
priorities.summary: Community and local funding theme with a dedicated manifesto section.

priorities.position: A safer Scotland; community police officers for every neighbourhood
priorities.summary: Public safety is a dedicated section and a clear campaign signal.

priorities.position: Protecting our natural environment; end the block on clean nuclear energy
priorities.summary: Environmental section with an explicit pro-clean-energy signal.

priorities.position: A government that works for you; end government waste and cut a third of quangos; right to recall MSPs
priorities.summary: Clear governance and anti-waste reform agenda.
Dedicated but lower-ranked international/outward-looking policy section.

notes: This JSON uses manifesto section page spans as a weighting proxy rather than exact word counts.
The contents page is the main structural source for weighting, while the headline plan pages were used to refine the ranking.
This file is suitable as a high-confidence manifesto-led input for the matcher.
